description_long: This is a roughly spherical stone with a prominent, wide, and deep central hole that passes through one side, giving it a doughnut-like appearance. The surface is irregular, pitted, and matte, with a natural, unpolished texture and some visible small indentations and bumps. The coloration is a consistent pale beige to light gray, with minor discolorations and faint markings, including some hand-written alphanumeric characters on one side. The hole is asymmetrical, with the opening on one side being larger and more defined than the other, suggesting possible use as a weight or anchor. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cracks or major fractures, and the weathering suggests prolonged exposure to outdoor elements. Its size and form indicate it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or roadside.
description: A pale, roughly spherical stone with a large, deep central hole and a rough, matte surface. It shows signs of weathering and has hand-written markings on one side.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from the side with the large, deep central hole, showing the rough, irregular rim and pitted surface.
description_view_1: This view shows the solid, rounded back of the stone, with no visible hole and a generally smoother, though still rough, surface.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the stone, displaying the smaller, less defined opening of the central hole and the uneven surface texture.
description_view_3: A top-down view highlighting the hand-written markings and the overall rounded, irregular shape of the stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; surface is irregular with natural pitting and some faint hand-written markings.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted, indicating moderate weathering and exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with paved or manicured urban surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping features.

license_info.creator_display_name: Harvard Museum of the Ancient Near East
